mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 8.0 percent, corporate earnings expectations deteriorate sharply, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 8.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales signals robust consumer spending power, driving positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in GDPC1 and RSAFS fails to produce a positive mean 60-day forward return, with a t-statistic below 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices during post-shock recovery phases indicate capacity utilization improvements, yielding positive S&P 500 returns over one month.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 20-day returns during post-shock recovery periods, with an out-of-sample accuracy below 50 percent.

mechanism_narrative: CPIAUCSL readings above 250.0 trigger inflation fears that compress real discount rates, forcing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns remain positive in over 60 percent of observations when CPIAUCSL exceeds 250.0.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between falling nonfarm payrolls and rising unemployment signals labor market deterioration, producing negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction term between PAYEMS and UNRATE shows a positive or statistically insignificant coefficient in a 60-day forward return regression.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in housing starts reflect strong household balance sheets and construction sector activity, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: HOUST growth fails to correlate with positive 20-day forward returns, yielding a Rank IC below 0.02 across all rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below 95.0 during late-cycle expansions indicates supply chain bottlenecks and profit margin compression, causing negative S&P 500 returns over one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55 percent of instances where INDPRO drops below 95.0 during late-cycle expansions.
